message:
refactor(server): hoist auth barrier and echo tool to module-level statics

Move AUTH_BARRIER OnceLock and auth_barrier_slot() from inside the
http_read_only_snapshot_keeps_request_auth_isolated test function up to
the module-level test statics section, alongside HTTP_OVERLAP_METRICS
and HTTP_OVERLAP_LOCK. Extract http_auth_echo_tool_runtime into a
standalone #[tool]-annotated function so it can be referenced as
HttpAuthEchoToolRuntime (the generated struct name) rather than being
re-declared inside the test body.

This eliminates a nested static + nested function pattern that prevented
the tool from being reused across tests and made the test body harder to
follow. The Barrier::new(3) in the auth_barrier_slot is corrected to
Barrier::new(2), matching the actual number of concurrent HTTP requests
the test dispatches (two overlapping auth-bearing requests).

Also in tasks.rs: capture running_snapshot.is_some() into should_start
before passing the snapshot by value to notify_snapshot(), fixing a
use-after-move where the is_none() check happened after the value had
already been consumed by the notification call.

In tests.rs: update three call sites from ActiveRequestGuard::new() to
ActiveRequestGuard::try_new().expect(), reflecting the API change from
infallible to fallible construction that returns an error when the
request ID is already tracked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
